text: During the laparoscopic procedure, the surgeon observes patent fallopian tubes and identifies endometriosis in the pouch of Douglas, which is then excised.
The surgeon injects vasopressin into the uterus, followed by making an oblique incision on the uterine surface. The fibroid is then enucleated from the uterine tissue.

The surgeon sutures the inner layer of the uterine defect in two layers using StrataFix barb suture, followed by suturing the serosa with interrupted polyglycine I sutures. The fibroid is mosellated within a containment bag and removed. Anti-adhesion gel is applied to the excised endometriotic site and the uterine incision.
A photograph taken during the caesarean section shows the healed myomectomy incision on the uterus. The image reveals no adhesions between the uterus and the abdominal wall or the omentum.
